pick.rationale: Lehigh holds significantly superior advanced metrics compared to Prairie View A&M, ranking 282nd in Torvik and 284th in KenPom versus Prairie View's 292nd/291st rankings. The Mountain Hawks' Patriot League pedigree combined with their better overall record (18-16 vs 17-17) and superior efficiency margin (-10.38 vs -11.1) give them a clear advantage in this First Four play-in. While both teams are ranked near the bottom of Division I, Lehigh's slightly stronger underlying performance indicators make them the more likely winner.

reasoningStep.title: CBB Ratings Comparison for South 16 Play-In
reasoningStep.summary: Evaluated Bart Torvik and KenPom ratings to determine statistical edge between two low-ranked teams.
reasoningStep.evidence: Lehigh: Torvik 282, KenPom 284, 18-16, AdjEM -10.38 | Prairie View A&M: Torvik 292, KenPom 291, 17-17, AdjEM -11.1

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: 2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026

Round: First Four

Prior committed picks: 3

I need to predict the winner of the current game: Lehigh (lehigh) vs Prairie View A&M (prairie-view-a-m) in the South 16 play-in (First Four).

Let me gather relevant information about both teams before making a pick. I should:
1. Look up their CBB ratings (Bart Torvik and KenPom)
2. Search for recent news/injury info if available
3. Consider conference pedigree, recent performance, and matchup specifics

Let me start by looking up their ratings.

Good, I have the ratings. Let me analyze:

Lehigh:
- Torvik rank: 282
- KenPom rank: 284
- Record: 18-16
- Conference: Patriot League (Pat/PL)
- AdjO: ~102.5-102.8 (ranked 289-290)
- AdjD: ~112.7-113.1 (ranked 262)
- AdjEM: -10.38

Prairie View A&M:
- Torvik rank: 292
- KenPom rank: 291
- Record: 17-17
- Conference: SWAC
- AdjO: ~101.0-101.1 (ranked 308-311)
- AdjD: ~111.9-112.2 (ranked 238)
- AdjEM: -11.1

Lehigh has better rankings across the board:
- Better Torvik rank (282 vs 292)
- Better KenPom rank (284 vs 291)
- Better offensive efficiency (102.5-102.8 vs 101.0-101.1)
- Slightly worse defensive efficiency but not by much
- Better AdjEM (-10.38 vs -11.1)
- Better record (18-16 vs 17-17)
- Patriot League is generally considered a stronger mid-major conference than the SWAC

Let me search for any recent news or injury information that might affect this matchup.

modelTrace.content:
The web searches are consistently failing with 403 errors. Let me try a different search query format or see if I can get any useful information another way.

Given the data I have from the CBB ratings, I can make a reasonable pick based on:
1. Lehigh has better rankings across all metrics (Torvik 282 vs 292, KenPom 284 vs 291)
2. Lehigh has a better record (18-16 vs 17-17)
3. Lehigh's conference (Patriot League) is generally considered stronger than SWAC
4. Lehigh has better offensive efficiency and slightly better AdjEM
5. Prairie View A&M actually has slightly better defensive efficiency rank (238 vs 262), but this doesn't offset their other deficiencies

Based on the pattern from my prior picks, I've been favoring teams with superior advanced metrics and conference pedigree. Lehigh clearly fits that profile here.

Let me make the pick for Lehigh with moderate confidence given how close these teams are in the ratings.

Good, I can see the South region teams. This confirms Lehigh and Prairie View A&M are in the First Four play-in for the South region.

Based on my analysis:
- Lehigh has superior rankings across all metrics (Torvik 282 vs 292, KenPom 284 vs 291)
- Lehigh has a better record (18-16 vs 17-17)
- Lehigh's Patriot League conference is generally considered stronger than SWAC
- Lehigh has better offensive efficiency and AdjEM (-10.38 vs -11.1)
- The only area where Prairie View A&M edges out is defensive efficiency rank (238 vs 262), but this is offset by their inferior offense

Following the same analytical approach as my prior picks (favoring superior advanced metrics and conference pedigree), Lehigh should win this matchup. However, given how close these teams are in the ratings (both are bottom-tier tournament teams), I'll keep confidence moderate at around 58-60%.

Let me finalize my pick.
